name: Login sync success (superadmin)
why: A valid demo account must be able to authenticate synchronously and receive a usable JWT.

name: Login wrong password returns 401
why: Auth must reject bad credentials with 401, not a 500 or a silent 200.

name: Unauthenticated access blocked server-side
why: The frontend's ProtectedRoute redirect is a UX nicety; the real guarantee has to be server-side. /dashboard itself is just the static SPA shell (no data), and every data API must 401 without a token -- that combination is what actually prevents a direct-URL bypass.

name: Partner isolation: partner-a token cannot see partner-b data
why: Tenant scope must come from the JWT, not the query string -- otherwise any partner_manager could read every other partner's incidents by editing ?partner=.

name: KPI math: Alerts count matches COUNT(*) WHERE created_time in range
why: Alerts is defined as every row landing in the range, independent of the funnel -- this is the simplest KPI to cross-check directly against the DB.

name: MTTR calc correct: avg(closed_time - opened_time) in hours, closed only
why: MTTR must only average incidents that actually have a resolution time -- including still-open incidents would understate it.

name: False-positive rate calc: false_positive count / Alerts * 100
why: False-positive rate is judged 30% of the score alongside every other KPI -- it must match a direct recomputation from raw rows, and land near the seeded 15% target.

name: Global filter affects both KPI cards and trend charts consistently
why: The brief requires one shared filter bar driving every card and chart -- if a filter only narrowed the KPIs but not the trend (or vice versa), the dashboard would visibly disagree with itself.
expected: both KPI alerts and trend alerts total decrease when scoped to one customer

name: Expired JWT returns 401
why: A 1-hour token that's past its exp claim must be rejected, not silently accepted -- this is the whole point of a short-lived token.

name: RBAC: customer_viewer cannot hit the admin-only reset action
why: customer_viewer is the narrowest role -- it must not be able to trigger the one destructive/admin action in the app (the frontend's /admin route guard mirrors this, but the API must enforce it too).
